title: pseudoselago guttata
intro: Pseudoselago guttata, also known as the spotted rock-jet or spotted selago, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae and is native to the Western Cape Province of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which are said to aid in treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Pseudoselago guttata include its potential to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe wounds and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
